claims.claim_text: The LFCS exam is a performance-based assessment.
claims.rationale: The Linux Foundation's LFCS instructions explicitly describe the exam as performance-based tasks solved on the Linux command line.

claims.claim_text: Setting the setgid bit on a shared directory causes newly created files to inherit the directory's group.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Users and Permissions
claims.rationale: GNU Coreutils documents this directory-setgid inheritance behavior directly, but gnu.org is off the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: A `Type=oneshot` systemd service without `RemainAfterExit=yes` may complete successfully and then appear dead or inactive rather than active.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Running Systems and systemd
claims.rationale: The systemd service documentation explicitly states that oneshot services without RemainAfterExit do not enter active state after running.

claims.claim_text: Cron jobs run with a limited default environment and are executed by `/bin/sh` unless `SHELL` is overridden.
claims.rationale: The Cronie crontab format documentation states which environment variables are set automatically and that commands run via `/bin/sh` or the configured SHELL.

claims.claim_text: Device names are unstable, so UUIDs are generally safer than device names in `/etc/fstab`.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Storage and Persistence
claims.rationale: The util-linux mount documentation explicitly warns that device names are unstable and recommends identifiers such as UUID.

claims.claim_text: `mount -a` mounts all fstab entries except those marked `noauto`.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Storage and Persistence
claims.rationale: The util-linux mount documentation defines `mount -a` in terms of mounting all matching fstab entries except those with `noauto`.

claims.claim_text: A bad `/etc/fstab` entry has caused real production outages.
claims.anchor_text: The `/etc/fstab` file has been a source of real production outages because a single bad persistence entry can affect boot behavior long after the original administrator leaves the terminal.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A single bad `/etc/fstab` entry can break mounts or affect boot behavior long after the change was made.
claims.rationale: The sentence makes an unsourced real-world outage claim without naming or sourcing a concrete incident, so the operational lesson should remain while the historical assertion is removed.

further_reading.title: Linux Foundation LFCS Important Instructions
further_reading.why_relevant: Authoritative source for LFCS exam format, timing, and performance-based structure.

further_reading.title: systemd.service unit configuration reference
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for `Type=oneshot`, `RemainAfterExit`, and service-state verification details.

further_reading.title: util-linux mount(8) reference
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for UUID-vs-device-name guidance and `mount -a` behavior when checking persistence.

notes: Worked examples, analogies, local lab setup steps, quiz prompts, and general pacing advice were not seeded. I only flagged externalized exam/manual claims and the single unsupported historical outage assertion.
